nodes.label: Core requirements work reliably
nodes.description: All spec items (blocks, pages, DnD, formatting, persistence) function without data loss.

nodes.label: Reviewable in two minutes
nodes.description: A reviewer can grasp the product immediately: create a page, edit blocks, refresh, content remains.

nodes.label: Visible decision process
nodes.description: README and decision graph explain tradeoffs, cuts, and rationale — not just the final code.

nodes.label: Block-based editor
nodes.description: Users can create, edit, and delete content blocks (text, headings, bullet lists at minimum).

nodes.label: Multiple pages
nodes.description: Users can create and navigate between pages.

nodes.label: Drag-and-drop reorder
nodes.description: Users can reorder blocks within a page via drag-and-drop.

nodes.label: Basic text formatting
nodes.description: Users can apply bold, italic, and similar inline formatting within blocks.

nodes.label: Survive page refresh
nodes.description: Content must persist across browser refresh. localStorage is sufficient.

nodes.label: Use Vite + React + TypeScript
nodes.description: Client-side SPA scaffold for fastest path to a working editor.
nodes.rationale: Avoids Next.js hydration complexity for a localStorage-only app. Speed over prestige.

nodes.label: Use BlockNote for the editor
nodes.description: Block-based editor library built on TipTap/ProseMirror instead of a custom contenteditable implementation.
nodes.rationale: Block editor from scratch is the highest schedule risk. BlockNote provides blocks, slash menu, DnD, and formatting.

nodes.label: Zustand + localStorage persistence
nodes.description: Zustand persist middleware writing to localStorage key wingrep-notes.
nodes.rationale: Minimal boilerplate, one source of truth, no backend required for core spec.

nodes.label: Tailwind CSS for styling
nodes.description: Utility-first CSS for fast layout and polish without a separate design system build-out.
nodes.rationale: Fast iteration on sidebar, page chrome, and empty states.

nodes.label: Flat block list for v1
nodes.description: No nested outline blocks in the first version; BlockNote default block tree only.
nodes.rationale: Nested bullets add editor complexity without being in the core spec. BlockNote handles list nesting internally if users indent.

nodes.label: Single-route SPA navigation
nodes.description: One URL with sidebar state for active page; no /p/:id routes in v1.
nodes.rationale: Faster to ship; URL routing is polish not a spec requirement.

nodes.label: Use BlockNote built-in drag handles
nodes.description: No custom @dnd-kit layer; rely on BlockNote's native block reordering.
nodes.rationale: Spike confirmed drag handles and reorder work out of the box. Avoids fighting editor state.

nodes.label: Editable page title in canvas header
nodes.description: Page title is a large input above the editor, synced with sidebar label.
nodes.user_problem: Users expect the document title at the top of the page like Notion.
nodes.chosen_behavior: Borderless text input above editor; updates page store on change.

nodes.label: Fixed left sidebar for pages
nodes.description: Persistent sidebar with page list and + New button.
nodes.user_problem: Users need quick navigation between multiple pages.
nodes.chosen_behavior: 256px left sidebar, active page highlighted, delete on hover.

nodes.label: Application state schema
nodes.description: Single source of truth for pages and active page id, persisted to localStorage.
nodes.contract: { pages: Page[], activePageId: string } where Page = { id, title, content: Block[], updatedAt }

nodes.label: Page store
nodes.description: Zustand store with persist middleware for pages, navigation, and content updates.

nodes.label: Page editor
nodes.description: BlockNote wrapper; syncs editor.document to page store on change.

nodes.label: Sidebar
nodes.description: Page list navigation, create and delete pages.

nodes.label: Page canvas
nodes.description: Main content area with title input and editor; waits for store hydration.

nodes.label: BlockNote covers required block types
nodes.description: BlockNote provides paragraph, heading, bullet, DnD, and inline formatting out of the box.

nodes.label: Custom editor blows timeline
nodes.description: Building a block editor from scratch could consume the entire 48h budget.

nodes.label: DnD fights editor internal state
nodes.description: Custom drag-and-drop integration may conflict with BlockNote's built-in reordering.

nodes.label: Schema changes break saved data
nodes.description: Evolving the app-state shape without migration logic could wipe user content on refresh.

nodes.label: AI feature eats polish time
nodes.description: Adding an AI integration before core requirements work could delay deploy and UX polish.

nodes.label: Does BlockNote cover all required block types and DnD?
nodes.description: Spike confirmed paragraph, heading, bullet, bold/italic, slash menu, and drag reorder.

nodes.label: Should blocks support nesting?
nodes.description: Deferred: flat v1 model; BlockNote native indent for lists is sufficient.

nodes.label: URL-based page navigation?
nodes.description: Deferred to v2; single-route SPA chosen for speed.

nodes.label: Ship AI in 48h or defer?
nodes.description: Whether to add a block-transforming AI feature or document it as v2.

nodes.label: Inline editable page title at top?
nodes.description: Yes — large canvas header input implemented.
